name: Accordion
description: Displays a list of collapsible items where users can expand or collapse content sections. Supports single or multiple open items simultaneously.
when_to_use: Presenting grouped content that users can expand on demand
Settings panels with optional detail views
avoid: When all content should always be visible (use plain sections)

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: Initial expanded item(s) for uncontrolled usage

name: ActionsPanel
description: Renders multiple action buttons in a row with an overflow dropdown for items that do not fit. Used for bulk actions in table or list selection contexts.
when_to_use: Bulk action toolbars in tables or lists
Showing selected item count with available actions

props.name: maxRowActions
props.description: Max visible actions before overflow menu

examples: <ActionsPanel actions={[{id: 'del', text: 'Delete', handler: () => {}}]} renderNote={() => '3 selected'} onClose={() => {}} />

name: ActionTooltip
description: A tooltip variant for labeling icon buttons with an optional description and keyboard shortcut. Provides accessible labeling for icon-only interactive elements.
when_to_use: Showing keyboard shortcuts alongside button labels
avoid: For general hover info on non-interactive elements (use Tooltip)

props.name: closeDelay
props.description: Ms to delay hiding after hover ends

props.name: openDelay
props.description: Ms to delay showing after hover begins

name: Alert
description: Displays inline status messages with themed styling for info, success, warning, danger, and other states. Supports optional close button, action buttons, and icon customization.
when_to_use: Inline status messages within page content
examples: <Alert theme="warning" title="Unsaved changes" message="Changes will be lost." onClose={() => {}} />
<Alert theme="danger" title="Error" message="Request failed." actions={[{text: 'Retry', handler: () => {}}]} />

name: ArrowToggle
description: A chevron arrow icon that rotates to indicate directional or expand/collapse state. Used inside collapsible triggers, dropdowns, and tree nodes.
avoid: As standalone clickable element (needs a Button wrapper)

name: Avatar
description: Displays a user avatar as an image, icon, or text initials. Supports multiple sizes, themes, shapes (circle/square), and custom colors.
when_to_use: Representing users in lists, headers, comments
avoid: For general image display (use img tag)

props.name: fallbackImgUrl
props.description: Fallback image URL if main fails

name: AvatarStack
description: Displays a horizontal stack of overlapping Avatar components with a counter or dots indicator when items exceed the maximum visible count.
when_to_use: Showing a group of users compactly
Team member displays in cards or headers
avoid: When individual user identity matters (show full User components)

props.name: max
props.description: Maximum avatars visible before overflow indicator

name: Box
description: A polymorphic layout block with built-in CSS property shortcuts for width, height, position, overflow, and spacing. Serves as the foundation for layout composition.
when_to_use: Applying layout CSS properties with spacing system

props.name: as
props.description: HTML element or component to render as

props.name: spacing
props.description: Spacing shortcuts: m, mx, my, mt, mb, ml, mr, p, px, py, pt, pb, pl, pr

name: Breadcrumbs
description: Navigation showing the current page's position in a hierarchy. Automatically collapses middle items into a dropdown when space is limited.
when_to_use: Page location within deep navigation hierarchies
Quick navigation back to parent pages

props.name: showRoot
props.description: Always show root item when collapsed

name: breakpoints
description: Default responsive breakpoints used by the layout system (Container, Row, Col, Flex). Values represent minimum viewport widths in pixels for each breakpoint.
when_to_use: Reference when building responsive layouts with layout components
Override via LayoutProvider to customize breakpoints
avoid: Hardcoding pixel values; use the Space scale or breakpoint names instead

name: Button
description: A versatile button that renders as a button, anchor, or custom element. Supports multiple visual variants, sizes, loading and disabled states, and icon slots.

props.name: component
props.description: Custom element type to render as

props.name: href
props.description: Renders as anchor with this href

name: Card
description: A surface container for grouping related content. Supports container, action, and selection types with various visual themes and views.
when_to_use: Selectable items in grids or lists

name: Checkbox
description: A checkbox input with checked, unchecked, indeterminate, and disabled states. Supports controlled and uncontrolled modes.
avoid: Mutually exclusive options (use Radio or RadioGroup)

name: ClipboardButton
description: A ready-to-use button that copies text to the clipboard with success/error tooltip feedback. Combines CopyToClipboard and ClipboardIcon.
when_to_use: Code blocks, API keys, URLs needing clipboard copy
Any copy action with visual feedback
avoid: When you need custom copy UI (use CopyToClipboard directly)

name: ClipboardIcon
description: An icon that changes based on clipboard status (pending/success/error). Used as a visual indicator inside CopyToClipboard render functions.
when_to_use: Inside custom clipboard copy components as a child of CopyToClipboard

name: Col
description: A responsive column for 12-column grid layouts. Must be used inside a Row component. Supports responsive sizing per breakpoint.
when_to_use: Building responsive grid layouts inside Row

props.name: size
props.description: Column span 1-12 or responsive size object

name: Container
description: Centers page content horizontally with configurable gutters and optional maximum width. Typically used once per page as the root layout wrapper.
when_to_use: Root layout wrapper for page content
Constraining content width on large screens
avoid: Repeated card containers (use Card or Box)

props.name: spaceRow
props.description: Vertical space between child Row components

name: CopyToClipboard
description: A headless wrapper that copies text to the clipboard and exposes operation status (pending/success/error) to its render-function children.
when_to_use: Building custom copy UI with status feedback
avoid: When pre-built copy button is sufficient (use ClipboardButton)

props.name: text
props.description: Text to copy or function returning text

props.name: timeout
props.description: Ms before status resets to pending

name: DefinitionList
description: Renders term-definition pairs in a structured list with optional copy, note, and separator styling. Supports horizontal and vertical layouts.
avoid: Tabular data with multiple columns (use Table)

props.name: contentMaxWidth
props.description: Max width of the value column in pixels

props.name: nameMaxWidth
props.description: Max width of the term column in pixels

name: Dialog
description: A modal overlay for important user interactions requiring focused attention or a decision. Supports Header, Body, Footer subcomponents with focus management.
examples:
<Dialog open={isOpen} onClose={() => setIsOpen(false)}>
  <Dialog.Header caption="Confirm" />
  <Dialog.Body>Are you sure?</Dialog.Body>
  <Dialog.Footer onClickButtonApply={handleOk} onClickButtonCancel={() => setIsOpen(false)} textButtonApply="OK" />
</Dialog>

name: Disclosure
description: A single collapsible section with summary and details subcomponents. Simpler than Accordion, for standalone expandable content blocks.

props.name: keepMounted
props.description: Keep content in DOM when collapsed

props.name: summary
props.description: Summary content when not using Disclosure.Summary

name: Divider
description: A horizontal or vertical separator line, optionally containing text or icon content centered in the line.
when_to_use: Between list items or menu groups
avoid: When semantic separation is not needed

props.name: children
props.description: Optional content embedded in the line

name: Drawer
description: A sliding panel that appears from any screen edge. Supports resizable panels with configurable min/max sizes and optional backdrop.
when_to_use: Context panels that should not block main content

name: DropdownMenu
description: A dropdown menu triggered by a button. Supports grouped items, submenus, icons, and custom trigger rendering.
when_to_use: Context menus on rows or cards

name: FileDropZone
description: A drag-and-drop file upload zone with configurable accept types, title, description, and upload button. Supports compound component pattern for custom layouts.
avoid: Simple file input (use Button with input type=file)

name: FilePreview
description: Displays a preview card for a File object with file type icon, name, description, and hover-revealed action buttons.
examples: <FilePreview file={file} actions={[{icon: <Icon data={Xmark} />, title: 'Remove', onClick: onRemove}]} />

name: Flex
description: A flexbox layout utility component with responsive media query support. Extends Box with flex-specific props for direction, alignment, wrapping, and gap.

name: HelpMark
description: A question-mark icon button that opens a Popover with contextual help text. Used for inline help tooltips next to form fields or labels.
when_to_use: Inline help next to form fields or labels
avoid: For long documentation (use a link instead)
When help is always visible (use a plain text hint)

props.name: popoverProps
props.description: Props forwarded to the internal Popover

name: Hotkey
description: Displays keyboard shortcuts with platform-appropriate key symbols (Cmd on Mac, Ctrl on PC). Supports light and dark view modes.
when_to_use: Displaying keyboard shortcuts in tooltips or documentation
avoid: For interactive keyboard handling (use event listeners)

name: Icon
description: An SVG icon wrapper component. Renders IconData (from @gravity-ui/icons) as an accessible inline SVG with configurable size, fill, and stroke.
when_to_use: Rendering any Gravity UI icon from @gravity-ui/icons
Custom SVG icons via IconData format

name: Label
description: A compact badge or tag for highlighting metadata, statuses, or key-value pairs. Supports copy, close, and info interaction types with multiple visual themes.
when_to_use: Showing status, category, or metadata tags
avoid: For long text content (use Text or Alert)

props.name: copyText
props.description: Text to copy (enables copy type behavior)

name: Link
description: A styled text link for navigation. Supports multiple visual views and visited state. Intended for inline text navigation links.
when_to_use: Navigation links in sentences or descriptions
avoid: Primary CTA buttons (use Button with href)

name: List
description: A virtualized scrollable list with built-in filtering, sorting, and keyboard navigation. Renders large item collections efficiently.
when_to_use: Large lists of items needing virtualization
avoid: Small static lists (use ul/li or Menu)

props.name: onItemClick
props.type: (item: T, index: number, fromKeyboard?: boolean) => void

props.name: renderItem
props.type: (item: T, isActive: boolean, itemIndex: number) => React.ReactNode

name: Loader
description: A global loading indicator showing animated bars. Used for page-level or dialog-level loading states. Unlike Spin, this is not for inline/button use.
avoid: Loading overlays on specific elements (use Overlay with Loader inside)

name: Menu
description: A list of action items with optional icons, groups, and interactive states. Used as the content inside dropdowns, context menus, and navigation panels.
examples:
<Menu size="m">
  <Menu.Group label="Actions">
    <Menu.Item iconStart={<Icon data={Pencil} />} onClick={onEdit}>Edit</Menu.Item>
    <Menu.Item theme="danger" onClick={onDelete}>Delete</Menu.Item>
  </Menu.Group>
</Menu>

name: Modal
description: A low-level modal overlay for pop-up windows. Provides backdrop, focus trap, portal rendering, and escape key handling. Use Dialog for pre-built layouts.
when_to_use: Building custom overlay content not covered by Dialog

name: NumberInput
description: A numeric text input with increment/decrement controls, min/max bounds, step, and decimal support. Inherits TextInput appearance with view, size, and pin props.

props.name: label
props.description: Label to the left of input

name: Overlay
description: Renders a semi-transparent overlay over the parent element (which must have position: relative). Used to show loading state while preserving layout.
when_to_use: Showing loading state over specific content sections
Preserving layout during async data fetching

name: Pagination
description: Page navigation controls with configurable page size options, page number input, and compact mode. Supports controlled and uncontrolled modes.
avoid: Small item counts that all fit on screen
examples: <Pagination page={currentPage} pageSize={20} total={items.length} onUpdate={(p, ps) => {setPage(p); setPageSize(ps);}} />

name: Palette
description: Displays a grid of selectable icons, emojis, reactions, or symbols. Supports multi-select, disabled states, and configurable column counts.

name: PasswordInput
description: A TextInput variant for passwords with reveal toggle and copy buttons. Inherits all TextInput props except type.
when_to_use: Sensitive information inputs with reveal toggle

name: PinInput
description: A group of character inputs for entering PINs, OTPs, or verification codes. Auto-advances focus on input and supports numeric or alphanumeric modes.
when_to_use: OTP or SMS verification code entry

props.name: mask
props.description: Mask values like a password field

props.name: onUpdateComplete
props.description: Fires when all inputs are filled

name: PlaceholderContainer
description: An empty state container with image, title, description, and action buttons. Used for zero-data states, error pages, and onboarding prompts.
examples:
<PlaceholderContainer
  title="No results found"
  description="Try different search terms"
  image={<NoResultsIllustration />}
  actions={[{text: 'Clear filters', view: 'normal', onClick: clearFilters}]}
/>

name: Popover
description: A floating content panel anchored to a trigger element. Supports hover/click trigger modes, arrow, delay, and safe polygon for mouse movement. Built on Popup.

props.name: enableSafePolygon
props.description: Prevents close when moving mouse to popover

name: Popup
description: A low-level floating content wrapper built on Floating UI. Provides positioning, anchor attachment, and portal rendering for building custom overlay components.
when_to_use: When Popover or Tooltip do not match requirements

props.name: anchorElement
props.description: Element to position popup relative to

name: Portal
description: A wrapper around React.createPortal that renders children into a DOM node outside the current component tree. Defaults to document.body.
when_to_use: Rendering floating elements outside parent DOM context
avoid: Unnecessary portal wrapping of static content

name: Progress
description: A horizontal progress bar displaying a value from 0-100. Supports themed colors, text labels (on size m), composite stacks, and color stop breakpoints.

props.name: text
props.description: Text label (only visible at size m)

name: Radio
description: A single radio button input. Usually used inside RadioGroup for mutually exclusive option sets. Supports controlled and uncontrolled modes with label content.
when_to_use: Inside RadioGroup for exclusive option selection
Single boolean option (when Checkbox does not fit)
avoid: Use standalone outside RadioGroup only when building custom grouping
examples:
<RadioGroup value={selected} onUpdate={setSelected}>
  <Radio value="a">Option A</Radio>
  <Radio value="b">Option B</Radio>
</RadioGroup>

name: RadioGroup
description: A group of Radio buttons for mutually exclusive selection. Supports horizontal/vertical layout, options array, and nested Radio subcomponents.
when_to_use: Mutually exclusive option selection in forms
avoid: When options look better as tabs (use SegmentedRadioGroup)

name: Row
description: Defines margins between Col components in a 12-column grid layout. Must wrap Col components and accepts space and spaceRow for gap control.
when_to_use: Wrapping Col components in a grid layout

props.name: space
props.description: Horizontal and vertical gap between columns

props.name: spaceRow
props.description: Override vertical gap when columns wrap

name: SegmentedRadioGroup
description: A group of mutually exclusive options styled as a segmented button strip. Visually similar to tabs but functions as radio buttons.
avoid: More than 5-6 options (use Select or RadioGroup)

name: Select
description: A feature-rich dropdown select component supporting single and multi-select, filtering, grouped options, and custom rendering. Replaces native select elements.
when_to_use: Dropdown selection from a list of options

props.name: options
props.description: Array of options or use Select.Option children

examples:
<Select
  options={[{value: 'a', content: 'Option A'}, {value: 'b', content: 'Option B'}]}
  value={selected}
  onUpdate={setSelected}
  placeholder="Choose..."
/>

<Select multiple filterable placeholder="Select tags">
  <Select.Option value="1">Tag 1</Select.Option>
  <Select.Option value="2">Tag 2</Select.Option>
</Select>

name: Sheet
description: A mobile bottom sheet component that slides up from the bottom. Supports drag-to-dismiss, dynamic resizing, scrollable content, and hash-based URL state.
when_to_use: Mobile context menus and action sheets

props.name: allowHideOnContentScroll
props.description: Allow swipe-down when content at top

name: Skeleton
description: A loading placeholder that mimics the shape of content before it loads. Supports gradient and pulse animation styles.
when_to_use: Showing content shape while data loads
Preventing layout shift during async loads

name: Slider
description: A range slider for selecting a value or range. Supports marks, custom tooltips, start point visualization, and discrete values via null step.
when_to_use: Numeric range selection with visual feedback

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: Initial value (number for single, tuple for range)

props.name: inverted
props.description: Fill from right instead of left

props.name: step
props.description: Step size (null for discrete marks only)

name: spacing
description: The spacing scale used by Box, Flex, and the spacing() utility. The base unit is 4px. Space values are multipliers: space 1 = 4px, space 4 = 16px, space 10 = 40px.
when_to_use: Setting consistent spacing via spacing props on Box/Flex
Using the spacing() utility for CSS class generation
avoid: Arbitrary pixel values; use the scale for consistency

name: Spin
description: A small inline rotating spinner for loading states within components. Unlike Loader, Spin is used in inline contexts such as buttons and labels.
when_to_use: Inline loading indicators next to text

name: Stepper
description: Displays progress through numbered wizard steps. Supports view states (idle/error/success) per step, custom icons, separator customization, and click navigation.

name: Switch
description: A toggle switch for binary on/off states. Supports controlled/uncontrolled modes, loading state, indeterminate state, and label content.
when_to_use: Immediate on/off settings without a save step
avoid: When change needs confirmation (use Checkbox + save button)

name: Table
description: A data table with column configuration, row selection, sorting, settings, copy, and action HOCs. Supports sticky scroll, row click handlers, and empty state message.
when_to_use: Structured data display with multiple columns
Tables with row actions or bulk selection
avoid: Small static lists (use List or Menu)

props.name: getRowId
props.description: Row ID field name or function

props.name: onRowClick
props.type: (item: any, index: number, event: React.MouseEvent) => void

examples:
const MyTable = withTableSorting(withTableSelection(Table));
<MyTable data={data} columns={columns} getRowId="id" selectedIds={selected} onSelectionChange={setSelected} />

name: TableColumnSetup
description: A popup UI for configuring table column visibility and order. Shows a button that opens a list of columns users can show/hide and optionally reorder via drag and drop.
when_to_use: Tables where users need to configure visible columns

props.name: items
props.description: Column items with id, title, selected, required

props.name: hideApplyButton
props.description: Apply immediately vs on Apply button

examples:
<TableColumnSetup
  items={columns.map(c => ({id: c.id, title: c.name, selected: visible.includes(c.id)}))}
  onUpdate={handleColumnsUpdate}
  sortable
/>

name: Text
description: A typography component for rendering text with design system variants, semantic colors, overflow handling, and polymorphic element support.
when_to_use: Any text content requiring design system typography
Text with overflow ellipsis or color semantics

props.name: color
props.description: Semantic text color (primary, secondary, complementary, info, positive, warning, danger, utility, etc.)

props.name: variant
props.description: Typography variant (body-1, body-2, body-3, caption-1, caption-2, display-1 through display-4, header-1, header-2, subheader-1 through subheader-3, code-1 through code-3)

examples: <Text variant="caption-2" color="secondary" ellipsis>Truncated secondary text that is too long</Text>

name: TextArea
description: A multi-line text input with auto-height, min/max rows, clear button, and validation state. Supports normal and clear views.
examples: <TextArea value={text} onUpdate={setText} minRows={3} maxRows={10} placeholder="Enter description" />

name: TextInput
description: A single-line text input field with optional label, start/end content, clear button, and validation state. The primary text form control in the design system.

props.name: endContent
props.description: Content after the input and clear button

props.name: label
props.description: Label to the left of input

props.name: startContent
props.description: Content before the label and input

examples: <TextInput label="Search" hasClear startContent={<Icon data={Magnifier} />} validationState="invalid" errorMessage="Required" />

name: Toaster
description: A notification toast system with ToasterProvider context, useToaster hook, and imperative Toaster class API. Supports themed toasts with actions, auto-dismiss, and updates.

props.name: autoHiding
props.description: Auto-hide delay in ms (false = no auto-hide)

name: Toc
description: A table of contents component showing two levels of page hierarchy. Items can be links or clickable buttons with an active item indicator.
when_to_use: In-page navigation for long documentation or articles

props.name: items
props.description: TOC items with value, content, href, and optional child items

examples:
<Toc
  value={activeSection}
  onUpdate={setActiveSection}
  items={[
    {value: 'intro', content: 'Introduction'},
    {value: 'usage', content: 'Usage', items: [{value: 'props', content: 'Props'}]},
  ]}
/>

name: Tooltip
description: A simple text tooltip that appears on hover/focus over its anchor element. A lightweight alternative to browser title attribute with proper styling and delay control.
when_to_use: Adding hover hints to elements with existing text (role='tooltip')
avoid: Rich content with interactive elements (use Popover)
examples:
<Tooltip content="Save file" role="label" openDelay={200}>
  <Button view="flat"><Icon data={FloppyDisk} /></Button>
</Tooltip>

name: User
description: Displays a user avatar with a text info block showing name and description. Wraps Avatar for the avatar slot with flexible sizing.
when_to_use: Showing user identity with name and description

props.name: avatar
props.description: Avatar configuration, image URL, or custom React element

examples:
<User
  avatar={{text: 'John Smith', theme: 'brand'}}
  name="John Smith"
  description="john@example.com"
  size="l"
/>

name: MobileProvider
description: Context provider that sets mobile mode and platform for all descendant components. Required for Sheet, mobile-aware components, and useMobile/usePlatform hooks.
when_to_use: Wrapping the app or a section that uses mobile-specific components
Enabling Sheet, mobile sheets, and mobile-aware behavior
Providing routing history/location context for mobile components

props.name: children
props.description: React tree to provide mobile context to

name: Tab
description: An individual tab item rendered as a button, anchor link, or custom component. Must be used inside TabList. Supports icon, counter, and label badge.
when_to_use: Rendering individual tabs inside a TabList
Tab navigation with icons, counters, or label badges
avoid: Complex page navigation (use Breadcrumbs or Menu)

props.name: children
props.description: Tab label text (falls back to value if omitted)

props.name: counter
props.description: Badge counter displayed inside the tab

props.name: href
props.description: Renders the tab as an anchor with this href

props.name: icon
props.description: Icon displayed before the tab label

props.name: label
props.description: Label badge shown after the tab title

examples:
<TabList value={active} onUpdate={setActive}>
  <Tab value="overview">Overview</Tab>
  <Tab value="logs" icon={<Icon data={Logs} />} counter={42}>Logs</Tab>
</TabList>

name: TabList
description: A container for Tab components that manages selection state, keyboard navigation, and sizing. Can be used standalone or inside a TabProvider for linked TabPanel content.
when_to_use: Standalone tab bar without associated panels
avoid: When content sections are unrelated (use separate navigation)
When tabs exceed 7-8 items (consider Select or overflow handling)

props.name: activateOnFocus
props.description: Activate tab on keyboard focus instead of requiring Enter

name: TabPanel
description: A content panel associated with a Tab via a matching value. Hides/shows based on the active tab value provided by TabProvider or TabList context.
when_to_use: Rendering content for a specific tab inside a TabProvider

props.name: value
props.description: Tab value this panel corresponds to

examples:
<TabProvider value={active} onUpdate={setActive}>
  <TabList>
    <Tab value="a">Tab A</Tab>
    <Tab value="b">Tab B</Tab>
  </TabList>
  <TabPanel value="a">Content A</TabPanel>
  <TabPanel value="b">Content B</TabPanel>
</TabProvider>

name: TabProvider
description: Context provider that synchronizes active tab state between a TabList and multiple TabPanel components. Manages a shared id, value, and onUpdate callback.
when_to_use: Connecting TabList with one or more TabPanel components
Sharing tab selection state across disconnected components
avoid: When only a TabList is needed with no panels
examples:
<TabProvider value={tab} onUpdate={setTab}>
  <TabList>
    <Tab value="settings">Settings</Tab>
    <Tab value="profile">Profile</Tab>
  </TabList>
  <TabPanel value="settings"><SettingsForm /></TabPanel>
  <TabPanel value="profile"><ProfileForm /></TabPanel>
</TabProvider>

name: UserLabel
description: A compact label chip for displaying users, emails, or generic entries with an avatar. Supports click and close interactions.
when_to_use: Closable user chips in filter bars

name: useToaster
description: Returns toast management methods (add, update, remove, removeAll) from the ToasterProvider context. The primary way to show toasts from React components.
when_to_use: Triggering toast notifications from React components
avoid: Outside React components (use the Toaster instance directly)
signature: useToaster(): { add: (toast: ToastOptions) => void; update: (name: string, options: Partial<ToastOptions>) => void; remove: (name: string) => void; removeAll: () => void; has: (name: string) => boolean; }

name: useActionHandlers
description: Returns an onKeyDown handler that calls the callback when Enter or Spacebar is pressed. Emulates native button keyboard behavior on custom interactive elements.
when_to_use: Making custom non-button elements respond to Enter/Spacebar for accessibility
Adding keyboard activation to div/span clickable elements
avoid: On actual <button> elements (already handles keyboard natively)
signature: useActionHandlers<T>(callback?: (...args: any[]) => any): { onKeyDown: React.KeyboardEventHandler<T> }

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Function to invoke when Enter or Spacebar is pressed

examples:
const {onKeyDown} = useActionHandlers(handleClick); return
Click me
;

name: useAsyncActionHandler
description: Wraps an async function to track its loading state. Returns the wrapped handler and an isLoading flag, preventing manual loading state management.
when_to_use: Button or form submit handlers that trigger async operations
Tracking loading state of any async function
avoid: Complex multi-step workflows (use dedicated state machines)
signature: useAsyncActionHandler<Action extends (...args: any[]) => PromiseLike<any>>({ handler }: { handler: Action }): { isLoading: boolean; handler: (...args: Parameters<Action>) => Promise<Awaited<ReturnType<Action>>> }
examples:
const {isLoading, handler: handleSave} = useAsyncActionHandler({handler: saveData});
<Button loading={isLoading} onClick={handleSave}>Save</Button>

name: useCollapseChildren
description: Measures how many children fit inside a container and returns the visible count. Used to build overflow-collapsing toolbars or breadcrumbs that hide items that don't fit.
when_to_use: Building toolbars that collapse overflowing items into a menu
Any container that needs to show as many items as fit
signature: useCollapseChildren(props: UseCollapseChildrenProps): { calculated: boolean; recalculate: () => void; visibleCount: number }
return_type: { calculated: boolean; recalculate: () => void; visibleCount: number }

parameters.name: preservedRefs
parameters.description: Refs of elements that always count toward the container width but are excluded from the count

parameters.name: maxCount
parameters.description: Maximum number of visible items (default: Infinity)

name: useColorGenerator
description: Generates a deterministic background color from a seed string (e.g., user name or id). Returns RGB, OKLCH, and a contrasting text color. Respects the current theme.
when_to_use: Generating consistent avatar background colors from user names or IDs
Assigning unique colors to entities without storing color data
avoid: When colors need to be user-customizable (use a color picker)
signature: useColorGenerator({ seed }: { seed: string }): { hash: number; oklch: { l: number; c: number; h: number }; rgb: { r: number; g: number; b: number }; textColor: string }

parameters.name: seed
parameters.description: Unique attribute to derive the color from (e.g., user name, id, email)

examples:
const {rgb, textColor} = useColorGenerator({seed: user.name});
<Avatar text={user.name} backgroundColor={`rgb(${rgb.r},${rgb.g},${rgb.b})`} color={textColor} />

name: useControlledState
description: Manages controlled and uncontrolled state in a single hook, mirroring React's pattern. Accepts a controlled value and a default value and returns current state plus a setter that calls onUpdate.
when_to_use: Building components that support both controlled and uncontrolled usage
Form control internals that need to manage their own state when no value prop is provided
avoid: Simple internal state with no external control needed (use useState)
signature: useControlledState<T, C = T, Args extends any[] = []>(value: T | undefined, defaultValue: T, onUpdate?: (v: C, ...args: Args) => void): [T, (value: C, ...args: Args) => void]

parameters.name: onUpdate
parameters.description: Callback called when the state changes

name: useFileInput
description: Shapes props for a hidden file input and a visible trigger element. Returns controlProps for the input and triggerProps (onClick) for the button.
when_to_use: Building custom file upload buttons without using native file inputs directly
Decoupling the file input from the visible trigger
signature: useFileInput({ onUpdate, onChange }: { onUpdate?: (files: File[]) => void; onChange?: (event: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void }): { controlProps: React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>; triggerProps: { onClick: () => void } }

parameters.name: onUpdate
parameters.description: Callback with the array of selected File objects

name: useFocusWithin
description: Tracks focus within a container element and its descendants. Returns focusWithinProps to spread onto the container. Calls callbacks when focus enters or leaves the subtree.
when_to_use: Closing a dropdown or popup when focus leaves the container
Highlighting a container when any child is focused
signature: useFocusWithin<T extends Element>(props: { isDisabled?: boolean; onFocusWithin?: (e: React.FocusEvent<T>) => void; onBlurWithin?: (e: React.FocusEvent<T>) => void; onFocusWithinChange?: (isFocusWithin: boolean) => void }): { focusWithinProps: { onFocus?: Function; onBlur?: Function } }

parameters.name: onFocusWithin
parameters.description: Called when the container or a descendant receives focus

parameters.name: onBlurWithin
parameters.description: Called when focus leaves the container and all descendants

parameters.name: onFocusWithinChange
parameters.description: Called with the current focus-within boolean state

name: useForkRef
description: Merges multiple React refs into a single ref callback. Useful for combining an external ref with an internal ref on the same element.
when_to_use: Forwarding a ref while also keeping an internal ref to the same element
Combining library refs with component-internal refs
avoid: When only one ref is needed (use it directly)
signature: useForkRef<T>(...refs: Array<React.Ref<T> | undefined>): React.RefCallback<T> | null

parameters.name: ...refs
parameters.description: Two or more refs to merge into one

name: useIntersection
description: Calls a callback when the target element enters the viewport using IntersectionObserver. Useful for lazy loading and infinite scroll triggers.
when_to_use: Triggering actions when an element scrolls into view
avoid: Polling scroll position (use this hook instead)
signature: useIntersection({ element, options, onIntersect }: { element: Element | null; options?: IntersectionObserverInit; onIntersect?: () => void }): void

parameters.name: onIntersect
parameters.description: Callback fired when the element intersects the viewport

name: useLayoutContext
description: Returns the active media query, theme, and helper functions (isMediaActive, getClosestMediaProps) from the layout context. Requires a ThemeProvider or LayoutProvider ancestor.
when_to_use: Reading the current breakpoint for adaptive rendering
Conditionally rendering components based on screen size
Getting responsive prop values from a breakpoint map
avoid: Without a ThemeProvider/LayoutProvider in the tree
signature: useLayoutContext(): { theme: LayoutTheme; activeMediaQuery: MediaType; isMediaActive: (size: MediaType) => boolean; getClosestMediaProps: <T>(map: Partial<Record<MediaType, T>>) => T | undefined }

name: useLayoutEffect
description: A safe version of React.useLayoutEffect that falls back to a no-op on the server (SSR). Use instead of React.useLayoutEffect to avoid SSR warnings.
when_to_use: DOM measurements or mutations that must run synchronously before paint
avoid: For data fetching or non-DOM side effects (use useEffect)
signature: useLayoutEffect(effect: React.EffectCallback, deps?: React.DependencyList): void

name: useListNavigation
description: Adds keyboard arrow navigation to a list of items attached to an anchor element. Returns the active item index and controls. Handles Up/Down/PageUp/PageDown/Home/End keys.
when_to_use: Dropdown menus, comboboxes, or listboxes needing keyboard navigation
Any list component that needs arrow-key item selection
avoid: Non-list interactive patterns (use useActionHandlers for Enter/Space only)
signature: useListNavigation<ItemType, AnchorType extends HTMLElement>(props: UseListNavigationProps<ItemType, AnchorType>): { activeItemIndex: number; setActiveItemIndex: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<number>>; reset: () => void }
return_type: { activeItemIndex: number; setActiveItemIndex: Dispatch; reset: () => void }

parameters.name: items
parameters.description: Array of list items to navigate

parameters.name: anchorRef
parameters.description: Ref to the element that receives keydown events

name: useMobile
description: Returns the current mobile boolean from MobileContext. True when MobileProvider is configured with mobile=true.
when_to_use: Conditionally rendering mobile vs desktop variants
Reading mobile state set by MobileProvider
avoid: Without a MobileProvider ancestor (always returns false)
signature: useMobile(): boolean

name: useOutsideClick
description: Calls a handler when a mouseup or touchend event occurs outside the referenced element. Used to dismiss popups, dropdowns, and modals on outside click.
when_to_use: Closing dropdowns or popovers when clicking outside
Detecting touches outside a floating element
avoid: For focus-based dismissal (use useFocusWithin with onBlurWithin)
signature: useOutsideClick<K extends HTMLElement>({ ref, handler }: { ref: React.RefObject<K | null>; handler?: (e: MouseEvent | TouchEvent) => void }): void

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: Ref to the element to consider 'inside'

name: usePlatform
description: Returns the current Platform value from MobileContext ('browser' | 'ios' | 'android'). Set via MobileProvider.
when_to_use: Adapting behavior or rendering for specific mobile platforms
signature: usePlatform(): Platform
examples:
const platform = usePlatform();
return platform === Platform.IOS ? <IOSButton /> : <DefaultButton />;

name: usePortalContainer
description: Returns the portal container element from PortalContext. Falls back to document.body. Used internally by Popup, Tooltip, and other floating components.
when_to_use: Getting the correct portal container when building custom floating elements
Respecting a custom portal target set by PortalProvider
signature: usePortalContainer(): HTMLElement | null
examples:
const container = usePortalContainer();
return ReactDOM.createPortal(<MyPopup />, container ?? document.body);

name: useResizeObserver
description: Calls a callback whenever the observed element changes size using ResizeObserver. Falls back to window resize event when ResizeObserver is unavailable.
when_to_use: Reacting to element size changes without polling
Triggering layout recalculation when a container resizes
signature: useResizeObserver<T extends Element>({ ref, onResize, box }: { ref: React.RefObject<T | null | undefined> | undefined; onResize: (info: { observer?: ResizeObserver }) => void; box?: ResizeObserverBoxOptions }): void

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: Ref to the element to observe

parameters.name: onResize
parameters.description: Callback invoked when the element's size changes

name: useSelect
description: Core selection logic for Select-like components. Manages open/close state and value selection (single or multiple). Returns handlers and state for building custom select controls.
when_to_use: Building a custom select or combobox that needs selection and open state management
avoid: When the standard Select component suffices (use Select directly)
signature: useSelect<T>(props: UseSelectProps): UseSelectResult<T>

parameters.name: defaultValue
parameters.description: Default selected values for uncontrolled mode

examples: const {value, isOpen, toggleOpen, handleSelection} = useSelect({defaultValue: [], multiple: false});

name: useTimeout
description: Declarative timeout that fires a callback after ms milliseconds. The timeout is cleared on unmount or when ms/callback change. Passing null or undefined disables the timeout.
when_to_use: Auto-dismissing notifications or toasts after a delay
Debounce-free delayed actions within React lifecycle
avoid: When manual control of start/clear is needed (use setTimeout directly)
signature: useTimeout(callback: () => void, ms: number | null | undefined): void

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Function to call after the timeout

parameters.name: ms
parameters.description: Delay in milliseconds. Pass null or undefined to disable.

name: useUniqId
description: Returns a stable unique id string per component instance. Uses React.useId when available, with a fallback for older React versions. Prefixed with the design system namespace.
when_to_use: Generating unique IDs for aria-labelledby, aria-describedby, htmlFor attributes
Any place a stable per-instance id is needed
avoid: IDs that must be user-controlled (accept id as a prop instead)
signature: useUniqId(): string
examples:
const id = useUniqId();
return <><label htmlFor={id}>Name</label><input id={id} /></>;

name: useViewportSize
description: Returns the current viewport width and height. Listens to the visual viewport resize event and updates on every change.
when_to_use: Reading viewport dimensions for custom responsive logic
Positioning elements relative to the viewport
signature: useViewportSize(): { width?: number; height?: number }

name: useVirtualElementRef
description: Creates a virtual element ref compatible with @floating-ui/react. The virtual element has a getBoundingClientRect based on the provided rect. Used for positioning popups at arbitrary coordinates.
when_to_use: Anchoring a Popup or Tooltip to a cursor position or arbitrary coordinates
Context menus that appear at the right-click position
avoid: When anchoring to a real DOM element (pass the element ref directly)
signature: useVirtualElementRef(props?: { rect?: { top?: number; right?: number; bottom?: number; left?: number } | null; contextElement?: Element }): React.MutableRefObject<VirtualElement>

parameters.name: rect
parameters.type: { top?: number; right?: number; bottom?: number; left?: number } | null | undefined
parameters.description: Position of the virtual element relative to the viewport

parameters.name: contextElement
parameters.description: DOM context element for the virtual element

examples:
const anchorRef = useVirtualElementRef({rect: {top: mouseY, left: mouseX, bottom: mouseY, right: mouseX}});
return <Popup anchorRef={anchorRef} open={open}><ContextMenu /></Popup>;

name: configure
description: Globally configures the uikit library (lang, fallbackLang). Notifies all active subscribers when called. Call once at app startup before rendering.
when_to_use: Setting the application language for uikit components at startup
avoid: Calling on every render (call once at app init)
signature: configure(newConfig: { lang?: string; fallbackLang?: string }): void

parameters.name: newConfig
parameters.description: Partial config object to merge into the global config

name: getComponentName
description: Returns the display name of a React component. Checks displayName, then name, and falls back to 'Component'.
when_to_use: Resolving a human-readable name for debugging or error messages
HOC wrappers that need to preserve or read the inner component name
avoid: Production UI rendering (names may be minified)
signature: getComponentName<T>(Component: React.ComponentType<T>): string

parameters.name: Component
parameters.description: The React component to get the name of

name: getConfig
description: Returns the current global uikit configuration object containing lang and fallbackLang.
when_to_use: Reading the current configured language in a component or utility
Checking the active language setting at runtime
avoid: When only setting the config (use configure)
signature: getConfig(): { lang: string; fallbackLang: string }

name: getUniqId
description: Returns a globally unique string ID prefixed with the design system namespace. Increments a counter on each call. Use useUniqId in React components; use getUniqId outside React.
when_to_use: Generating unique IDs outside of React components (e.g., in utilities or stores)
Assigning stable IDs to data items
avoid: Inside React components (use useUniqId for stability across renders)
signature: getUniqId(): string

name: spacing
description: Generates a space-separated string of CSS class names from margin/padding shorthand props (m, mt, mb, ml, mr, mx, my, p, pt, pb, pl, pr, px, py). Use to apply spacing tokens on non-Box elements.
when_to_use: Applying spacing tokens to elements that don't use Box or Flex
Combining spacing classes with other classNames
avoid: On Box or Flex components (use their m/p props directly)
signature: spacing(props: SpacingProps, className?: string): string

parameters.name: props
parameters.description: Object with spacing prop keys: m, mt, mb, ml, mr, mx, my, p, pt, pb, pl, pr, px, py. Values are Space tokens.

parameters.name: className
parameters.description: Optional additional class name appended to the result
